race.name: Alutaguse Maraton
race.tagline: Forty-two kilometers through the wilderness of Ida-Virumaa — Estonia's largest Estoloppet event where ancient bog trails and dark spruce forest deliver the most remote skiing in the Baltic states.

climate.description: February in Ida-Virumaa brings some of Estonia's most reliable winter conditions. The northeastern location, close to the Russian border, has a more continental climate than western Estonia. Temperatures typically -12°C to -3°C. Better snow reliability than coastal areas.
challenges: Remote location in eastern Estonia limits access options
Flat bogland terrain demands sustained effort with no hills for variety
Baltic snow reliability still less certain than Scandinavian venues
Frozen bog can create exposed, windy sections

course.primary: Course through the Alutaguse wilderness in Ida-Virumaa, Estonia's easternmost region. The terrain crosses raised bogs, pine forest, and spruce woodland in one of the most remote areas of the Baltic states. Flat to gently rolling — classic Estonian geography but in a wilder, less developed setting than other Estoloppet venues.
features: Alutaguse is Estonia's largest and most remote wilderness area
Part of the Estoloppet series — Estonia's national ski marathon circuit
Largest field size among Estoloppet events at ~500 participants
Ancient raised bogs and dark spruce forest landscape
Near the Russian border — Estonia's easternmost wilderness
Both classic and skate technique available

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: The largest Estoloppet event by field size with a genuinely wild setting. Remote Alutaguse wilderness gives strong scenery marks for the Baltic context. Better snow reliability than western Estonia due to continental influence. Limited international profile and flat terrain keep overall score moderate.

history.summary: The Alutaguse Maraton is the flagship event of the Estoloppet series, drawing the largest field of any Estonian ski marathon. Held in the Alutaguse wilderness of Ida-Virumaa — Estonia's easternmost region near the Russian border — the race traverses some of the most remote and wild landscape in the Baltic states. The area's raised bogs, ancient spruce forests, and minimal human development create a genuine wilderness skiing experience.
notable_facts: Largest field in the Estoloppet series at approximately 500 participants
Alutaguse is Estonia's largest wilderness area
Ida-Virumaa borders Russia — the easternmost point of the EU
Estonian raised bogs can be thousands of years old
Part of the Estoloppet series — Estonia's national ski marathon circuit
The region's continental climate provides better snow than coastal Estonia

videos.title: Highlights from Alutaguse Maraton 2026
videos.description:
Highlights from Alutaguse Maraton 2026

The Ski Classics Challengers event, Alutaguse Maraton, took place last Sunday with a 45km long-distance ski race in classic technique in Vasavere, Estonia. Watch the highlights here!

Video: Stuudiopunkt

videos.title: Alutaguse maraton
videos.description:
First win for the team!

“Hard to complain if you win," said Mart after the first victory in Alutaguse Marathon🥇. Good race from all the teammates. Warm-up is done - now we are ready to take on the challenge to win the 47. Tartu Maraton 😎

videos.title: Alutaguse maraton, 300 m 42 km stardist, 7.02.2015, Pannjärve

quotes.text: I like the course especially we went to the lakes and that was pretty fun. They say that Sam Marathon has a very very beautiful track. This is also true with your eyes.

The track was really really beautiful and there is so much snow here so it was pretty enjoyable. When the uphill started double polling was definitely the slowest version but they managed to do the whole race double bowling.
It was a fun course with turns and ups and downs and high speed all the time. I really had to dig deep to get the victory but it was fun to race there.
